lever: rank-revealing pivoted QR low-rank pseudoinverse fast path for m >= n

profile_target.profile_observation: The benchmark matrix is numerically rank 4, so the prior full-rank inverse lever correctly rejected; the hot cost was full 192x192 Gram Jacobi work for a low-rank matrix.

baseline_rch.command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-9gvse-clean-baseline rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/pinv_192x192_f64 --sample-size 10

candidate_rch.command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-9gvse-lowrank-bench2 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/pinv_192x192_f64 --sample-size 10

rejected_attempts.lever: certified square full-rank inverse fast path
rejected_attempts.reason: Score below 2.0 and wrong primitive for target: singular-value probe showed sigma_min ~= 1.28e-17, cond2 ~= 7.71e18, rcond*cond2 ~= 7711, rank above cutoff = 4. The full-rank certificate correctly rejected the benchmark matrix.

isomorphism_proof.semantics: For finite m>=n inputs, the fast path forms a pivoted modified-Gram-Schmidt QR of A P = Q R and only returns when residual Frobenius trace is <= 0.5 * rcond^2 * sigma_max(R)^2. Because the residual operator norm is bounded by Frobenius trace, all omitted singular directions are below the same SVD cutoff used by pinv. The result is P R+ Q^T, with R+ computed from the small R R^T eigensystem using the same cutoff. Otherwise the existing Gram path runs.
isomorphism_proof.ordering_and_ties: Pivot selection scans left-to-right and swaps only on strictly greater residual norm, so equal-norm tie order is deterministic and stable.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: The fast path has deterministic accumulation order. It is gated by a residual certificate and preserves NaN/non-finite/rcond-zero/full-rank fallback behavior by returning None to the existing Gram implementation.

verification: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax pinv_low_rank_qr -- --nocapture
